publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-12-02 00:17:31 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: David Gries papers
did.abstract: David Gries (April 26, 1939- ) is an American computer scientist at Cornell University. The David Gries papers (1956-2022, bulk 1964-2019) contains correspondence, publications and manuscripts, technical reports, algorithms, grants, and awards and degrees.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: David Gries (April 26, 1939- ) is an American computer scientist mainly known for his books The Science of Programming (1981) and A Logical Approach to Discrete Math (1993, with Fred B. Schneider). His research interests include compiler writing, programming methodology and related areas such as programming languages, related semantics, and logic. Gries was an advocate of teaching formal methods in programming as a core computer science topic and teaching it to undergraduates, a stance that found large amounts of debate within the computer science education community. In any case, Gries' reputation as a teacher was such that around 700 students and fellow faculty members were in attendance for his final lecture, given to his "Programming and Data Structures" class, in May 2022.

He earned a Bachelor of Science from Queens College in 1960. He spent the next two years working as a programmer-mathematician for the U.S. Naval Weapons Laboratory. He earned as Master of Science in mathematics from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champagne in 1963. While at Illinois, Gries worked with Manfred Paul and Ruediger Wiehle to write a full compiler for the language ALGOL 60 for the IBM 7090 mainframe computer. He earned his Dr. rer. Nat in 1966 from the Technical University of Munich, studying under Friedrich L. Bauer and Josef Stoer.

Gries was an assistant professor at Stanford University from 1966-1969 before becoming an associate professor at Cornell University. He spent the next 20 years at Cornell, including time as Chair of the Computer Science Department from 1982 to 1987. He spent 1999 to 2002 at the University of Georgia in Athens and returned to Cornell in January 2003. He was associate Dean for Undergraduate Programs in the Cornell University College of Engineering from 2003 to 2011. He was one of the first ten Weiss Presidential Fellows at Cornell.

Gries is author, co-author, or editor of seven textbooks and 75 research papers. His 1971 work Compiler Construction for Digital Computers was the first textbook to be published on designing and implementing language compilers. It was also one of the first textbooks to be written and produced using computers, in this case punched cards input to a text-formatting program that ran on an IBM System/360 Model 65. The early technology used eventually resulted in the book having a somewhat dated appearance. Compiler Construction for Digital Computers sold well and went through more than twenty printings. His other textbooks include An Introduction to Programming – A Structured Approach Using PL/I, PL/C, and PL/C7 (1973, co-written with Richard W. Conway), Programming Methodology: A Collection of Articles by Members of IFIP WG3.2 (1979), The Science of Programming (1981), and A Logical Approach to Discrete Math (1993). The textbook An Introduction to Programming: A Structured Approach, Using PL/I, PL/C, and PL/C7 used the PL/C dialect developed at Cornell and went through several editions and adaptations. It stressed the discipline of structured programming throughout, becoming one of the most prominent textbooks to do so, and introduced considerations of program correctness, becoming the first introductory textbook to do so.

Gries served as Chair of the Computing Research Board in the late 1980s, spearheading its morph into the Computing Research Association – opening an office in Washington, D.C. and beginning to actively represent the research and education interests of Computer Science there. He was also active in editorial duties. After about 40 years as editor and managing co-editor of Information Processing Letters, he was given the title of Honorary Editor-in-Chief. He has also been editor of the Springer-Verlag Series on Text and Monographs in Computer Science since 1973.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The David Gries papers (1956-2022, bulk 1964-2019) contains personal and professional correspondence with notable computer scientists; publications and manuscripts of articles, lectures, and reviews; technical reports; algorithms; grant proposals and projects reports; and awards, degrees, and fellowship materials. Collection also includes materials related to the editing and publication of Gries's many textbooks and computer science guidebooks, inlcuding an original manuscript of Compiler Construction for Digital Computers and an ALCOR-ILLINOIS control dictionary.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Gries papers, #16-13-4524. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to box 11 (grants and grant proposals) restricted to the permission of the University Archivist.

controlaccess.subject: Computer science
ALGOL (Computer program language)
IBM 7090 (Computer) Programming
Computer science -- Study and teaching. -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.

Series II. Publications
Subseries IIa. Textbooks
Compiler Construction for Digital Computers manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript for textbook printed using a punch card input to a text-formatting program on an IBM System/360 Model 65 printed on dot matrix paper. Includes a copy of the Cornell Keypunch Translation Program by Fred Zappert and Paul Reilly, May 1970.

did.unittitle: Compiler Construction for Digital Computers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and reviews of Gries' textbook, "Complier Construction for Digital Computers"

did.unittitle: A Logical Approach to Discrete Math

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, publishing agreements, reviews for the book, and anonymous student course reviews for CS 280 Discrete Math, taught by Gries. Also includes reviews of other textbooks written by Gries.

did.unittitle: Programming Methodology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Gries' editing of " Programming Methodology: a Collection of Articles by Members of IFIP WG2.3"

did.unittitle: ProgramLive

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publishing agreements, correspondence, and reviewer comments for "A multimedia introduction to programming using Java" and "ProgramLive" CD-ROM by David and Paul Gries.

did.unittitle: The Science of Programming

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and reviews of Gries' book, "The Science of Programming."

scopecontent.p: A contribution for the book "The Impact of Research on Software Technology," Eds. P. Wegner and W. Wulf. Includes a revision of the chapter in December 1976 and comments and rebuttals on the chapter between Gries and David Parnas. Also published as Cornell University Department of Computer Science Technical Report.

did.unittitle: Referee Report correspondence
Subseries IIIb. Grants and Grant Proposals

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permission of the University Archivist.

did.unittitle: Grant proposals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume. Contains materials for multiple accepted and rejected National Science Foundation and U.S. Navy grants. Includes grants for Research Support for Young Computer Science Faculty, Programming Methodology, and Programming Languages and Systems.

did.unittitle: CISE Educational Infrastructure: Revitalizing the Computer Science Curriculum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proposals, correspondence, and project reports.

did.unittitle: Development of a Teaching Laboratory for an Introductory Programming Course

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes materials related to proposals for Teaching Calculation and Discrimination (1990), a publishing agreement for "Programming in the 1990s: An Introduction to the Calculation of Programs," and Exploring Calculational Logic (1997)

did.unittitle: Programming Languages and Systems Programming
Software Engineering with a Transformational System

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: NSF Proposal ID# CCR-9503319. Includes materials for a proposal submitted by Jayadev Misra and the University of Texas at Austin titled "Loosely-Coupled Processes" (1990-1991).

did.unittitle: National Science Foundation proposals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials for various grants. Folder 6 includes materials for Programming Methodology, Research Experiences for Undergraduates, and Development and Implementation of a New Language/A New Programming Language and Environment. Folder 7 includes materials for A Distributed Computing Facility.

did.unittitle: Series IV. Awards, Degrees, Honors, and Jobs
Subseries IVa. Jobs, promotions, degrees
U.S. Naval Weapons Lab, Dhalgren, VA
University of Illinois Masters of Science degree
Technical University of Munich Dr.rer.nat degree
Cornell University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original offer letter from Cornell (1969), election as Associate Professor of Computer Science (1972), and appointment as the William L. Lewis Professor of Engineering (1991). Includes correspondence from Oregon State University regarding Gries' position as courtesy faculty (1997).

did.unittitle: University of Texas at Austin Regents' Chair in Computer Sciences

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from the University of Texas at Austin regarding Gries' visiting professorship as the Regents' Chair in Computer Sciences.

did.unittitle: University of Georgia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Gries' position as a Professor of Computer Sciences at the University of Georgia

did.unittitle: Subseries IVb. Awards, honors, and fellowships
Flushing High School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: High school degree and pamphlet from commencement.

did.unittitle: Eagle Scout, Boy Scouts of America
Queens College Certificate of Athletic Award
Association for Computing Machinery Programming Systems and Languages Paper Award

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Award presented for paper, "An Axiomatic Proof Technique for Parallel Programs I" co-authored by Gries and Susan Owicki.

did.unittitle: AFIPS Representative to IFIP TC-2
Guggenheim Fellowship
American Federation of Information Processing Societies awards
American Federation of Information Processing Societies Education Award
Cornell University John M. Clarke Award
American Association for the Advancement of Science Fellow

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from the American Association for the Advancement of Science regarding Gries' election as a Fellow.

did.unittitle: Computing Research Association Award for Service to Computing Research
Association for Computing Machinery SIGCSE Award for Outstanding Contributions to Computer Science Education
Cornell University Weiss Presidential Fellow

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings

did.unittitle: University of Wisconsin-La Crosse Distinguished Lecturer in Computer Science
Association of Computer Science Undergraduates Faculty of the Year Award
The Computer Society Taylor L. Booth Education Award
Association for Computing Machinery Fellow
Association for Computing Machinery Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award
Daniel Webster College Honorary Doctor of Laws

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and a pamphlet from Fall Semester 1996 convocation.

did.unittitle: Miami University, Honorary Doctor of Science
Jiangxi Normal University Consulting Professor
Amity University Honorary Professorship
Amity University Amity Booker's Prize for Academic Excellence in Java Programming

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Award given for the 2004 text "Multimedia Introduction to Programming using Java," which included a CD called "ProgramLive" that contained 250 short recorded lectures.

did.unittitle: Cornell University Certificate of Recognition as Outstanding Educator
SIGCSE Top Ten Symposium Papers of All Time

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For the paper "Using Software Testing to move Sudents from Trial-and-Error to Reflection-in-Action"

did.unittitle: Cornell University Class of 2019 Award
Cornell University Tau Beta Pi Professor of the Year Award
